channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Austria's new government is stopping family reunions immediately for migrants
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The new Austrian government said Wednesday that family reunion procedures for migrants will be immediately halted because the country is no longer able to absorb newcomers adequately.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and PHILIPP JENNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion program that keeps aging affordable housing livable
item.description: The Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing, threatening projects that keep tens of thousands of units livable for low-income Americans, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Schools use AI to monitor kids, hoping to prevent violence. Our investigation found security risks
item.description: One student asked a search engine, “Why does my boyfriend hit me?” Another threatened suicide in an email to an unrequited love. A gay teen opened up in an online diary about struggles with homophobic parents, writing they just wanted to be themselves.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE BRYAN of The Seattle Times and SHARON LURYE of The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis marks important milestones this week as he recovers from pneumonia
item.description: The recovery of Pope Francis from double pneumonia continued Wednesday as he inched toward notching some important milestones in his papacy: The 12th anniversary Thursday of his election as pope and a near-record in hospital stays for a pontiff.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Supporters of detained Columbia student arrested after clash with NYPD as Trump admin seeks his deportation
item.description: About a dozen protesters demonstrating in support of former Columbia University student Mahmoud Khalil were arrested on Tuesday as they clashed with police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hotel horror stories emerge after American college student goes missing as resort insists no connection
item.description: The Dominican Republic resort where University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki went missing experienced a lengthy power outage just hours before Virginia resident vanished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beth Holloway identifies key difference between Sudiksha Konanki case and her daughter's
item.description: The mother of an American teenager who was killed while vacationing in Aruba said the search for the 20-year-old college student who went missing in the Dominican Republic has one important distinction
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump officially increased tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25% on Wednesday, promising that the taxes would help create U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Manhunt underway for Philly driver who opened fire on teen, woman in road rage incident
item.description: A manhunt is underway for a driver accused of shooting a 15-year-old boy and a 20-year-old woman after an apparent road rage incident in West Philadelphia, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia won't retaliate against 'unjustified' US tariffs on steel and aluminum
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Wednesday that U.S. tariffs on Australian steel and aluminum were unjustified, but his government would not retaliate with its own tariffs. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A baby sea lion performs rhythmic gymnastics feats in Washington state
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A baby sea lion toting an artificial kelp strip was filmed performing intricate rhythmic gymnastics-esque circles through the waters of an aquarium in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court stays execution of Texas man days before he was set to die by lethal injection
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas appeals court on Tuesday halted the execution of a man who has spent more than 30 years on death row and had been set to die by lethal injection this week over the killings of six girls and young women found buried in the desert near El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal court hearing set in Columbia student's detainment over pro-Palestinian protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Manhattan federal court hearing is scheduled Wednesday in a suit challenging the detention of Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia University graduate student that the Trump Administration is trying to deport over his participation in pro-Palestinian protests at the school.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil didn't wear a mask. He's now the face of Trump's crackdown on campus protests
item.description: When protests over the Israel-Hamas war took root on Columbia University’s campus last spring, Mahmoud Khalil became a familiar, outspoken figure in a student movement that soon spread to other U.S. colleges.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz And Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: States should scale back abortion reporting demands, advocacy group says
item.description: An advocacy group says it's time for states to stop requiring abortion providers to file reports for every abortion.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House pressures Columbia University as it seeks to deport pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House complained Tuesday that Columbia University is refusing to help federal agents find people being sought as part of the government’s effort to deport participants in pro-Palestinian demonstrations, as the administration continued to punish the school by yanking federal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Blizzard Entertainment president says 'there's a game for you' no matter what type of player you are
item.description: IRVINE, Calif. (AP) — California gaming giant Blizzard Entertainment announced Thursday that its popular event BlizzCon is coming back after several years off. The celebration of all things Blizzard, which will be held at the Anaheim Convention Center, is scheduled for September 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Stroller accessory recalled after 6 children suffer finger lacerations: CPSC
item.description: Consumers who may have purchased or installed the accessories are being instructed to immediately discontinue use.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canada slaps electricity tariffs on New York, Minnesota, Michigan
item.description: This surcharge will affect electricity sales for 1.5 million homes and businesses across Michigan, Minnesota and New York,
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WGN 9

item.title: Fishermen pull 'wrapped' body out of water off coast of Massachusetts
item.description: The crew of the boat had reported finding the body in its fishing nets about 40 miles from the Massachusetts coast on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rubio confirms ICE arrest of pro-Palestinian Columbia student protest leader
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has confirmed the arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a green card-holding Columbia University graduate and pro-Palestinian activist, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court takes up Colorado conversion therapy case
item.description: The Supreme Court granted a Colorado therapist’s request to review her challenge to a state law banning mental health care providers from engaging minors in conversion therapy, a discredited practice that attempts to change a person’s sexual orientation or gender identity to align with heterosexual
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, ABC 4

item.title: Bills reward Allen with new contract after NFL MVP season. Deal is worth $330M, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — NFL MVP Josh Allen was rewarded Sunday with a contract extension worth $330 million, including an NFL record $250 million of it guaranteed, which makes him among the league’s highest-paid players.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who recently traveled abroad
item.description: Early symptoms include a fever higher than 101 degrees, a runny nose, cough and red, watery eyes. A person will experience a red rash one to four days after early symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans worry Trump's tariffs could harm economy
item.description: Republican lawmakers are growing alarmed over signs that President Trump’s expanding trade war is hurting the economy, something they’re hearing from constituents at home who are struggling to adapt to Trump’s zigzagging tariff pronouncements.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christopher Bell holds off Denny Hamlin at Phoenix to win third straight NASCAR Cup Series race
item.description: Bell survived a late drag race with Hamlin to become the first NASCAR Cup Series driver to win three straight races in the NextGen car, edging his teammate by 0.049 seconds to win the second-closest race in Phoenix Raceway history on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its 80,000 employees
item.description: The mass email went out to a “broad population of HHS employees," landing in their inboxes days before agency heads are due to offer plans for shrinking their workforces.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A total lunar eclipse and blood moon will be visible in March: How and when to watch
item.description: A treat awaits sky gazers in the month of March – the moon will take on a deep, reddish hue as it slips into the Earth's shadow during a total lunar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4
